raw:
I'll verify the current state of the work before issuing a reviewer verdict. Let me look at the repo and the relevant files.
The founder asked me to re-run the pre-ship reviewer ONLY and emit a clean verdict. Let me independently verify the work before approving — I won't make code changes. Let me read all the relevant files.
diff.ts matches spec verbatim, imports only `./memory.ts`. Now let me check learn.ts and index.ts.
Now let me read the test file and run both test suites to confirm green.
Now let me run both tests and verify clean imports to independently confirm green before issuing a verdict.
Both green. Now let me verify clean module imports and that the diff module has no cross-extension imports, plus check the test is executable.
All claims independently verified. Let me do a final reviewer-level check on the spec-critical details: known-only `fg` tokens, widget key/placement consistency, and timer reset/clear wiring.
The warning-path grep returned nothing due to my regex (the call spans template literals). Let me confirm the warning paths and the verbatim `theme.fg` valid tokens directly.
The theme tokens come from the pi package, not the repo. Since `fg()` throws on unknown tokens, let me confirm `toolDiffAdded`/`toolDiffRemoved`/`accent`/`dim` are all valid foreground tokens in the installed package.
Let me check the theme module directly to confirm the four tokens are valid and that `fg()` throws on unknown tokens.
Confirmed `fg()` throws on unknown tokens. Now let me verify the four tokens used (`toolDiffAdded`, `toolDiffRemoved`, `accent`, `dim`) are all registered in the theme's color map.
All four tokens are valid theme keys — no throw risk. Let me run a final colored-render smoke test against the real theme and check for any configured linter.
Everything is independently verified green against the real installed pi package. Final reviewer checklist complete:

- **diff.ts** — verbatim; exports `diffLearnedMarkdown`, `diffLearnedSections`, `isEmptyDiff`, `renderDiffLines`; imports **only** `./memory.ts` (node-free, no cross-extension imports).
- **learn.ts** — `runLearningPass` snapshots `beforeMarkdown` after `ensureLearnedScaffold` and re-reads `afterMarkdown` on `exitCode===0`, both in `try/catch` (best-effort, never throws); attaches optional `diff?: LearnedDiff`; index refresh / reason / updaterText / deltaCount unchanged.
- **index.ts** — success path → `showDiffWidget` → `ctx.ui?.setWidget?.("continual-learning-diff", lines, { placement: "aboveEditor" })`; palette uses `toolDiffAdded`/`toolDiffRemoved`/`accent`/`dim` — **all four confirmed valid `fgColors` keys**, and `fg()` throws only on unknown tokens (theme.js:251), so no throw risk; identity fallback when theme/fg absent; 30s `setTimeout` auto-dismiss with a shared timer reset on each pass; `pi.on("agent_start")` clears widget+timer immediately; empty/unavailable diff → `notify("info")`; failed/busy paths → `notify("warning")`; manual command mirrors it; all `ctx.ui` calls optional-chained.
- **Tests** — `diff_test.sh` (executable) → `diff_test: ALL PASS`; `learn_test.sh` → `learn_test: ALL PASS`. Reword surfaces as a single `changed` pair (`+1 ~1 -1`), `isEmptyDiff` true on identical, `renderDiffLines` respects `maxLines` with `+N more` footer. Colored-render smoke against a strict throw-on-unknown `fg()` passes.

The prior pass's only defect was an unparseable verdict; the implementation needs no changes.

REVIEW: APPROVE

The continual-learning "show what was learned" diff display is correct, complete, and green. Verified: `diff.ts` is verbatim and node-free (memory.ts-only import); `learn.ts` best-effort before/after snapshots attach `diff?: LearnedDiff` with existing fields intact; `index.ts` renders the auto-dismissing `aboveEditor` widget with valid theme tokens (no `fg()` throw risk), 30s timer + `agent_start` clear, notify fallbacks on empty/failed, mirrored in the manual command, all `ctx.ui` optional-chained; both headless test suites pass. Ship it.
